title: Ootoola

'''Ootoola''' was the vastly [[Ocean/Legends|oceanic]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Ootoolan]] race. The world was located in the [[Morshdine sector/Legends|Morshdine sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].

==Description==
{{Quote|...One -- stop calling them «fishfaces». They don't appreciate it, ...|[[Naradan D'ulin]], to [[Vilmarh Grahrk]]|<ref name="Deal" />}}
[[File:Ootoolan2.jpg|thumb|left|180px|An [[Ootoolan]].]]
A beautiful [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] world dominated by unusual twisted rock formations. The natives of the planet, an [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibious]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] [[Ootoolan]]s, built their cities within the rocks.<ref name="CEII" /> Their skin was a mottled bright green, and their heads were adorned with a fin-like ridge and several finned jowls. Outside the planet, they were sometimes derogatorily referred to simply as «Fishfaces» due to their appearance.<ref name="CEI" />

There were various [[Fish/Legends|fishes]] dwelling in the waters of Ootoola.<ref name="Deal" />

==History==
{{Quote|Lovely planet Ootoola, full of Fishfaces, ruled by a Patriarch who is king and priest both. Some Fishfaces say Patriarch not pure enough. Kill Patriarch. Kill wifey. Seize the throne.|[[Vilmarh Grahrk]]|<ref name="Deal" />}}
[[File:Ootoola_Central_Palace.jpg|right|thumb|250px|The coup on Ootoola]]
Over the [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Ootoola had developed a [[Theocracy|theocratic]] [[King/Legends|monarchy]], where one man – the patriarch – ruled as both king and priest. The ruler was expected to strictly adhere to their laws, thereby achieving a certain level of purity.<ref name="CEI" /> But in the final decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="NEGC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> a group of radical [[Ootoolan]]s accused the ruling patriarch of not being «pure» enough. They formed a group called the [[Revolutionary Purist Council]], hired assassins, and killed the patriarch and his wife. After that, they took over the throne and chose a new, «pure» leader. During the chaos, [[Princess/Legends|princess]] [[Foolookoola]], the only surviving member of the [[Royal family of Ootoola|royal family]], managed to escape.<ref name="Deal" />

Even though Ootoola was part of the Galactic Republic, the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] decided not to get involved, saying it was an internal matter. The [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] was ready to help, but the new patriarch — who supported the rebels — refused to let them visit the planet. With no one left to turn to, the loyalists of the old monarchy secretly planned to get Foolookoola off the planet. Her relatives and guardian, [[Naradan D'ulin]], reached out to a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named [[Vilmarh Grahrk]] to help.<ref name="Deal" />

At first, the escape seemed like it would go smoothly. But when Grahrk attacked the guards, he drew too much attention. Once they reached [[Inferno (Vilmarh Grahrk)|the ship]], he came up with a distraction: he aimed the ship’s weapons at the [[Central Palace]] and blew it up. In the confusion that followed, Foolookoola and her companions escaped to a nearby planet [[Dur Sabon]], where she would wait for the right moment to return home.<ref name="Deal" />

In the wake of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] obtained a rare edition of the ''Imperial Handbook'', and distributed it among key personnel such as [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]. He annotated the text with assessments of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] materiel and tactics, and discussed [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] missions that led to the destruction of an [[IM-S-653 Orbital Garrison]] over the planet Ootoola.<ref name="IH:ACG">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>
